attributes.content:
## Overview

The Review card gives the workspace a structured home for rating and review with score, text, author, date. Links to the reviewed entity.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Review anchors identity, permissions, or relationship context. It becomes most useful when other cards can link to it consistently, query against it safely, and inherit its context without duplicating sensitive or fast-changing information.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Organization]]** -- Review frequently sits inside an account, company, or team-level relationship context.
- **[[Contact]]** -- Review often needs a person-level reference for ownership, communication, or accountability.
- **[[Task]]** -- Review often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Deal]]** -- Deal can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Review.
- **[[Case]]** -- Case can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Review.
- **[[Email Template]]** -- Email Template can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Review.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Review as the structured home for rating and review with score, text, author, date. Links to the reviewed entity and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Review when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Review stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Review
cardInfo.summary: Rating and review with score, text, author, date. Links to the reviewed entity.
